extracted_rules.resource: Right to recover money from sick livestock transactions
verbatim: So, first of all, dealing entirely through you with Hegemachus about the deposit of the horse, I wished to cancel the transaction as the horse was in a sickly state: Diodorus here tried to dissuade me, asserting that Polycles would make no objection to refunding the twelve minae. So he said at the time; but after the death of the horse he ranged himself in the end with these men as my opponent, saying that I had no right to recover the money.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The passage describes a commercial dispute regarding a 'deposit' (transaction) for a horse. The disagreement centers on whether a buyer has the legal or normative right to cancel a sale and recover their twelve minae (a significant sum of money) if the animal is found to be sickly, illustrating a rule regarding property transactions and consumer protection among the Athenian wealthy class.

extracted_rules.resource: Access to arbitration for civil disputes
verbatim: This became evident: in the presence of the arbitrators Polycles angrily said that even my friends considered that I was in the wrong,—so they told him.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The mention of 'arbitrators' in a dispute over a financial deposit reflects the Athenian legal practice where civil cases were often first heard by public or private arbitrators before reaching a jury court. This represents a resource (legal recourse) available to citizens to settle disputes.

verbatim: My informant himself reported that you declared you would hinder those who intended to speak on my behalf, and had prevented several others already. What need have I to set the proof of these facts in a yet clearer light? I ask you, could that man know that, having asked Cleitodicus to speak next, I was refused?
extracted_rules.reasoning: In Athenian law, litigants had the right to ask friends or capable speakers (synegoroi) to speak on their behalf. The text shows this was a standard practice/right (as the speaker is aggrieved it was blocked), confirming that citizens had access to the resource of collective legal advocacy.
